title: synandra hispidula
intro: Synandra hispidula, also known as Rough-leaf Synandra, is a species of flowering plant in the family Pentaphylacaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia and has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and acting as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Synandra hispidula include tea infusions and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
